title: Movie Apocalypse: 'Trolls World Tour' Is the Most Important Film of 2020 | The Big Picture
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins on Universal dumping Trolls World Tour straight to $19.99 rental instead of theaters, then bring in Rob Harvilla to work out why a kids' movie is secretly a fight about rockism.

segments.heading: The movie apocalypse
segments.note: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins on the first blockbuster to skip theaters

films.title: Trolls World Tour
films.note: The first movie planned for a wide theatrical release that Universal sent straight to $19.99 home rental during the shutdown. Roughly $90M to make plus $30M in marketing, per Deadline. Universal told the trades it out-rented the digital opening day of Jurassic World: Fallen Kingdom tenfold; Sean and Amanda note the only source for that number is Universal, and doubt the release model survives past this one strange moment.

films.title: Trolls
films.note: The 2016 original, which both hosts watched for the first time this week (a jukebox musical their colleague Jason Gallagher warned is a parent's nightmare by the 30th 6:30 a.m. rewatch). Its actual lesson, they decide, is that drugs are bad: the Bergens eat trolls to feel happy until they learn happiness comes from within.

segments.heading: Into the trolls
segments.note: Rob Harvilla on a kids' movie about music criticism

films.title: Almost Famous
films.note: Harvilla's No. 1 among movies explicitly about rock criticism, with Trolls World Tour landing a distant third behind it. Poppy's pop trolls versus Rachel Bloom's Queen Barb and her hard-rock crusade is, he argues, a straight dramatization of the rockism-vs-poptimism debate Kelefa Sanneh took mainstream in the mid-2000s.

films.title: High Fidelity
films.note: His No. 2, which he admits he still relates to too closely: ahead of Trolls World Tour, it's the only movie he's watched that's theoretically about his own job, and one he hopes is the last.

films.title: Rock Dog
films.note: The one about a Tibetan-monastery guard dog groomed to fight wolves who instead wants an AC/DC-type band, and eventually repels the wolves with rock. Harvilla's Exhibit A for the strange genre of kids' movies that weaponize music history at his children.

films.title: Hotel Transylvania 3: Summer Vacation
films.note: Where Adam Sandler's vampire clan defeats a sea monster by DJ-ing, settling on the Macarena: Harvilla's other chewing-ice-in-the-dark low point, and a warning about earworms getting implanted in kids for months.

films.title: Sing
films.note: A Christmas Eve karaoke-competition movie that sent his kids into a months-long Baby Got Back loop, his shorthand for how these films regurgitate pop music at children.

films: The Lego Movie 2: The Second Part
